title: permission denied: OCI error unless sudo
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. centos stream 8 EC2 us-west-2 2. install podman from standard repo using @continaer-tools 3. try and run any -it image without sudo I rebuilt this machine just to try and isolate this issue and address it, I can't find a way to address these OCI errors without running the image as sudo/root. The "--security-opt label=disable" doesn't help. **Describe the results you received:** 
podman run -it alpine:latest ash                                                                                        144ms  Mon 12 Jul 2021 11:52:17 PM UTC
Error: container_linux.go:370: starting container process caused: process_linux.go:459: container init caused: process_linux.go:422: setting cgroup config for procHooks process caused: failed to write "2048" to "/sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1001.slice/user@1001.service/user.slice/libpod-9d309fe1d527053d598bfc12d77c19b208d5619b81484601622b4c17323e3c4e.scope/pids.max": open /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1001.slice/user@1001.service/user.slice/libpod-9d309fe1d527053d598bfc12d77c19b208d5619b81484601622b4c17323e3c4e.scope/pids.max: permission denied: OCI permission denied
**Describe the results you expected:** Like it seems to work for most, running a container without needing to sudo is my goal. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 3.1.0-dev API Version: 3.1.0-dev Go Version: go1.16.1 Built: Fri Mar 26 18:32:03 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.19.8 cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.0.27-1.module_el8.5.0+733+9bb5dffa.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.27, commit: dc08a6edf03cc2dadfe803eac14b896b44cc4721' cpus: 4 distribution: distribution: '"centos"' version: "8" eventLogger: file hostname: ip-172-199-2-149.us-west-2.compute.internal idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1001 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 165536 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1001 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 165536 size: 65536 kernel: 4.18.0-315.el8.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic memFree: 11424665600 memTotal: 16290885632 ociRuntime: name: runc package: runc-1.0.0-70.rc92.module_el8.5.0+733+9bb5dffa.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/runc version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.2-dev' os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true selinuxEnabled: true slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.1.8-1.module_el8.5.0+733+9bb5dffa.x86_64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.1.8 commit: d361001f495417b880f20329121e3aa431a8f90f libslirp: 4.3.1 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.1 swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 2h 29m 32.83s (Approximately 0.08 days) registries: search: - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.redhat.io - docker.io store: configFile: /home/secops-jzamani/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 8 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 8 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/secops-jzamani/.local/share/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 2 runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers volumePath: /home/secops-jzamani/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.1.0-dev Built: 1616783523 BuiltTime: Fri Mar 26 18:32:03 2021 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.16.1 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.1.0-dev ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` podman-3.1.0-0.13.module_el8.5.0+733+9bb5dffa.x86_64 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** Yes/No **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: `podman info` says this is a v2 system, but I didn't know that Cent Stream shipped that by default. Have you made any cgroups changes to convert the system to v2?

@mheon I checked the system and it was v2 enabled, so I modified the grub configuration according to <https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/world-domination-cgroups-rhel-8-welcome-cgroups-v2> in order to move to cgroupv2.

Also tried with 'setsebool -P container_manage_cgroup true' or 'setsebool -P container_manage_cgroup false' and received same result.

it is an issue with systemd on RHEL: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1897579

A workaround is to not set any pids limit: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9410#issuecomment-785840320

I am closing the bug as it is already tracked in bugzilla for the downstream.
